children.heading: .—Air carrier
children.label: 1 .—Air carrier
children.text: The term “air carrier” means any air carrier holding a certificate of public convenience and necessity issued by the Secretary of Transportation under section 41102.

children.heading: .—Aircraft purchase
children.label: 2 .—Aircraft purchase
children.text: The term “aircraft purchase” means the purchase of commercial transport aircraft, including spare parts normally associated with the aircraft.

children.heading: .—Capital reserve subsidy amount
children.label: 3 .—Capital reserve subsidy amount
children.text: The term “capital reserve subsidy amount” means the amount of budget authority sufficient to cover estimated long-term cost to the United States Government of a Federal credit instrument, calculated on a net present value basis, excluding administrative costs and any incidental effects on Government receipts or outlays in accordance with provisions of the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990 ( et seq.).2 U.S.C. 661

children.heading: .—Commuter air carrier
children.label: 4 .—Commuter air carrier
children.text: The term “commuter air carrier” means an air carrier that primarily operates aircraft designed to have a maximum passenger seating capacity of 75 or less in accordance with published flight schedules.

children.heading: .—Federal credit instrument
children.label: 5 .—Federal credit instrument
children.text: The term “Federal credit instrument” means a secured loan, loan guarantee, or line of credit authorized to be made under this subchapter.

children.heading: .—Financial obligation
children.label: 6 .—Financial obligation
children.text: The term “financial obligation” means any note, bond, debenture, or other debt obligation issued by an obligor in connection with the financing of an aircraft purchase, other than a Federal credit instrument.

children.label: A
children.text: a qualified retirement plan (as defined in section 4974(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) that is a qualified institutional buyer; and

children.label: B
children.text: a governmental plan (as defined in section 414(d) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) that is a qualified institutional buyer.

children.heading: .—Line of credit
children.label: 8 .—Line of credit
children.text: The term “line of credit” means an agreement entered into by the Secretary with an obligor under section 41763(d) to provide a direct loan at a future date upon the occurrence of certain events.

children.heading: .—Loan guarantee
children.label: 9 .—Loan guarantee
children.text: The term “loan guarantee” means any guarantee or other pledge by the Secretary under section 41763(c) to pay all or part of any of the principal of and interest on a loan or other debt obligation issued by an obligor and funded by a lender.

children.heading: .—New entrant air carrier
children.label: 10 .—New entrant air carrier
children.text: The term “new entrant air carrier” means an air carrier that has been providing air transportation according to a published schedule for less than 5 years, including any person that has received authority from the Secretary to provide air transportation but is not providing air transportation.

children.heading: .—Obligor
children.label: 11 .—Obligor
children.text: The term “obligor” means a party primarily liable for payment of the principal of or interest on a Federal credit instrument, which party may be a corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, or governmental entity, agency, or instrumentality.

children.label: B
children.text: designed to have a maximum passenger seating capacity of not less than 30 nor more than 75.

children.heading: .—Secured loan
children.label: 13 .—Secured loan
children.text: The term “secured loan” means a direct loan funded by the Secretary in connection with the financing of an aircraft purchase under section 41763(b).

children.label: A
children.text: is served (as determined by the Secretary) by a nonhub airport or a small hub airport;

children.label: B
children.text: is not within a 40-mile radius of an airport that each year has at least .25 percent of the total annual boardings in the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary determines does not have sufficient air service.
